name: Hao Pu (Zitai)

sections.short-biography: Tricked by Lü Meng into giving up his city, later forced to commit suicide.

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Hao Pu, styled Zitai, was from Yiyang commandery.

Not much is known about his early life, other than that he chose to serve Liu Bei instead of Cao Cao or Sun Quan when Jing province was divided. Around the early 210s, Hao Pu followed Liu Bei into Shu, and then made the Grand Administrator of Lingling.

When Lü Meng invaded Jing province against Liu Bei in 214, Changsha and Guiyang commanderies initially surrendered to him, but Hao Pu refused. Lü Meng tricked him by telling him through his old acquaintance Deng Xuanzhi that Lingling was doomed, as Liu Bei and Guan Yu had their own military problems. Hao Pu thus surrendered to Wu. Later, Lü Meng revealed the ploy, causing Hao Pu to be filled with regret since Liu Bei and Guan Yu could have saved Lingling.

He was said to be returned back to Liu Bei with Lingling commandery after Liu Bei and Sun Quan made peace. However, this either never happened or he surrendered again when Lü Meng invaded Jing province again in 219, as he would go on to serve Wu as Minister of Justice.

He was appointed Minister of Justice some point before 230. In 230, Yin Fan came from Wei to serve Wu, and Hao Pu grew close with him as Yin Fan showed interest working in the justice bureau. Hao Pu even complained that Yin Fan was being treated poorly, presumably advocating for promotions to be given to him, and claiming that he had the talent of a close royal advisor. However, Hao Pu misplaced his trust. Yin Fan was a spy for Wei who planned to sow discord in Wu, and attempted to rebel. When Yin Fan was found out and eliminated, Sun Quan personally rebuked Hao Pu, saying angrily, “Sir, you previously gave effusive praises to Yin Fan and also complained about the imperial court on his behalf. You made Yin Fan rebel. This is all your doing.” Hao Pu committed suicide soon after, implied to be forced.

full_text:
Hao Pu (Zitai) 郝普 (子太)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Hao Pu (郝普), styledZitai (子太), was born in Yiyang commandery. Tricked by Lü Meng into giving up his city, later forced to commit suicide. Hao Pu was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom and the Wu Kingdom. Hao Pu lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Hao Pu, styled Zitai, was from Yiyang commandery.
